defs: One of the antipodes; anything exactly opposite.
quotes: In tale or history your beggar is ever the just antipode to your king.
notes: ☞ The singular, antipode, is exceptional in formation, but has been used by good writers. Its regular English plural would be n"t*pōdes, the last syllable rhyming with abodes, and this pronunciation is sometimes heard. The plural form (originally a Latin word without a singular) is in common use, and is pronounced, after the English method of Latin, n*tp"*dēz.
